title: ABM acceptance rate runs 19 points higher than non-ABM leads
anchor: ABM leads convert to SDR acceptance at 61.4% on 70 leads this month.
connect: Non-ABM leads convert at 42.1% on 76 leads over the same window.
body: ABM leads convert to SDR acceptance at 61.4% on 70 leads this month. Non-ABM leads convert at 42.1% on 76 leads over the same window.
trace: ABM versus non-ABM acceptance rate gap shows targeting precision at the MQL-to-SQL handoff.

title: ABM and non-ABM lead volumes run nearly even this month
anchor: ABM generated 70 MQLs in April while non-ABM channels produced 76.
connect: The split sits at 48% ABM versus 52% non-ABM across total lead volume.
body: ABM generated 70 MQLs in April while non-ABM channels produced 76. The split sits at 48% ABM versus 52% non-ABM across total lead volume.
trace: April ABM versus non-ABM volume balance at 70 versus 76 MQLs shows channel mix.
